name: AJA DataCalc
desc: AJA’s DataCalc software computes storage or time requirements for professional video and audio media based upon codec choices, frame sizes and framerates. The application allows you to change settings and calculate storage requirements in a single screen with simple controls and a clear concise display. Summary information obtained by DataCalc can even be optionally emailed to team mates and a new Favorites feature allows you to save common calculations for speedy reference.

name: Mini-Matrix
desc: AJA Mini-Matrix is an interactive, searchable catalog of AJA Video and Audio Converter products for anyone needing to convert between different video formats and connection types. Designed to help you find the solution you need as fast as possible, whether on set or active in the field, the AJA Mini-Matrix's simple and powerful search tool gets you the answer you need. Search by name, input type, conversion type or output type or any combination.

name: Alford LED Wall Calculator
desc: The Alford LED Wall Calculator is designed to quickly and conveniently configure the panel requirements for numerous types of LED wall options. Many of the latest manufacturers and panel models are incorporated into the database, and we will be adding additional models as they become available. There is also an option to submit any new panels that are not currently in the app.

name: Alford Preset Remote
desc:
This app from Alford Media, sponsored by Barco, is your wireless preset controller for Barco’s Event Master’s family of switchers. With the Alford Preset Remote, Event Master's line of products is now at your fingertips on your iPad or iPad Mini.
With this update, there is now two-way communication between the app and the console. Every change and edit you make, whether on the app or on the console, will sync automatically to both devices. This feature allows more ease and flexibility in your programming.
It’s totally customizable and loaded with features, so you can work the way that best fits you. Features include the ability to Save, Edit, Custom Order and Restore Order. With Edit, you can edit the color and name on each preset button in the app. You can also re-sequence the preset buttons in the app by holding them down and dragging them to the desired location and you can reset to default settings with the touch of a button. The app also has buttons for Cut and an Auto-trans and the current preset is easily visible. For easier organization and access to you settings, the preset button setting can be saved to a show file within the app.
Please note: The app does not go to sleep because it is being used in a live event situation, so it is recommended that you plug in your iPad or iPad Mini whenever possible to maintain a charge.

name: Alford Widescreen Calculator
desc:
The Alford Media Widescreen Calculator is like having an Alford Video Engineer or Projectionist in your pocket! We have taken the chore of calculating overlapping widescreen projection math and made it touch screen simple. Just enter the size of your screen and the resolution of your projector and let the app tell you everything else you need to know.
It will instantly generate the Projected Ratio, Total Horizontal Pixels, Total Vertical Pixels, Number of Projectors, Overlap and Projected Width per Projector in Feet. Once you have the hard numbers just click on the Aim Points button to see a graphic representation of how your screens and projectors will line up and overlap. It’s just that easy. You can save the calculations directly to your iPhone Camera Roll as a screenshot from both the Results page and the Aim Points page. Keep the images for future reference or send them to colleagues via E-mail or SMS.

name: ArrayCalc Viewer
desc: The d&b ArrayCalc Viewer app provides all project data for rigging a d&b audiotechnik loudspeaker system. Once the system has been designed, calculated and optimized in the ArrayCalc simulation software, the relevant project information can be shared via email, Dropbox or USB cable

name: Audio Spectrum Analyzer dB RTA
desc: Spectrum Analyzer is a powerful real-time audio analysis app. Ideal for room tuning or speaker tuning, this app provides precision audio measurement and visualization that fits in your pocket!

name: AV Aid toolkit
desc:
Useful tools for the Audio Visual technician!

When you connect your device with a VGA or HDMI cable to a projector or flatscreen monitor, AV Aid will detect and display all available output resolutions for your device. You can then select one of these custom resolutions and AV Aid will generate and output color bars.

name: AV Buddy
desc: AV Buddy is a essential information and calculation tool which will assist your Audio Visual needs. Including pinouts, charts, cut lengths, diagrams, clear AV Connector photos, descriptions, AV Calculations. Each connector has a description with useful information.

name: AV Tools
desc: A simple but very useful collection of audio-visual tools and information for use when ever you need.

name: BLENDZ
desc: BlendZ was written as a quick widescreen calculator.

name: Bridle
desc: We combined something as basic as gravity and physics with the power of modern technology, because we feel that a swipe using multi touch technology means more than a thousand key strokes.

name: Decibel : dB sound level meter
desc:
Decibel Meter Pro is full of features sound level meter with exclusive accuracy of measurements and integrated spectrum analyzer. Calibrated with professional dB meter.

The world is a noisy place: cars, power tools, crowds, machinery, and other common sources produce amounts of noise that can, with continued exposure, lead to permanent hearing loss. Sound level meters are useful for anyone who wants to avoid exposing their ears to harmful noise levels, but who wants to spend hundreds of dollars on one?

dB Meter is free app which provides professional-quality measurement results, calibrated using an actual Nor140 high-precision decibel meter. With dB Meter, you can measure ambient noise levels, save your measurements and their locations, and share your measurements with others. dB Meter couples utility with stylish design and a polished user experience: a powerful tool in a sleek package, and at a tiny fraction of the cost of professional sound level meters.

(Please note that dB Meter soundmeter app is not a replacement for professional devices and should be used for personal purposes only.)

name: DMX Dip
desc:
DMX Dip allows you to easily calculate the correct address setting on your fixture.
DMX Dip saves you valuable time, you don't have to calculate what dip switches you have to set to put your fixture on an address. Typing three numbers into the app is much faster than calculating all that binary numbers in your head.
The reverse operation is also possible, just use the dip switches in the app like you would use real-life switches, and DMX Dip tells you exactly for which address the fixture is configured.

The offset feature comes in handy if you have to address the same fixture over and over again. Need to address two dozen LED-PARs? Set the offset to 4 and with the press of a single button you get all those addresses in no time.

name: DMXCalc
desc: DMXCalc helps you with all basic DMX address arithmetic for entertainment lighting system setup.

name: Electrical Formulas
desc:
Electrical Formulas is an app for anybody that needs to convert the following:

kVA - Amps - Single and three phase.
Amps - kVA - Single and three phase.

kW - kVA
kVA - kW

Electrical Formulas also has a Voltage Drop calculator and covers both mm2 and AWG cables for both single and three phase supplies.

name: Event Production Directory
desc: Online and in print, the Event Production Directory is the most widely-distributed event production resource of its kind, and now all the company information you expect from the EPD is conveniently available to you in the palm of your hand! The EPD app is the essential source for locating any type of live event production company in North America and beyond! You can conveniently search by category (lighting, sound, staging & more!), location (near you, in a specific city or within a defined radius), company name, and type of gear. With over 4,000 listings and growing, the EPD app is an essential tool for any serious production professional.

name: Go Button
desc: Play music and sound effects for your live show! From the makers of QLab.

name: GrandMA Remote
desc: The grandMA consoles are the core of the fully integrated MA system. The grandMA consoles are able to control – in the most elegant manner – moving lights, conventional lights, and effects, as well as LEDs and video. The reliable and proven operational philosophy allows a direct, flexible and intuitive way of working.

name: GRIDZ
desc:
A long time ago we wanted a program to generate grids for use for projection line up.

A company we used to use a lot had one but would not sell it to us to use, and so i made my own.

Originally it just generated grids for a single screen, then i was asked to make it handle blended screens, and then to have the ability to generate a lineup grid for times when you had to throw away pixels.

name: LensPro
desc: LensPro is the ideal companion of every projectionist.

name: Light Meter
desc: Lenspro allows the calculations of lenses:

name: Lighting Handbook
desc: Lighting handbook provides detailed information on nearly 1000 moving lights, consoles, dimmers, effects, smoke machines and more. It also provides pinout information for the most popular connectors used in the industry. Download product manuals for offline viewing, access 3 phase colour information, industry links and lots more!

name: M2 Calculator
desc:
The M2 Processor (by Brompton*) is used on Large LED video screen, it takes in a variety of inputs and has 4 output ports that can drive up to a maximum of 500,000 pixels per port, but there are some conditions. Frame Rate, Colour Depth and how many pixels each connected tile has can impact the number of tiles you can drive per Port.

By selecting the type of LED tile in the wall (Currently CT only has the Roe MC7HB the Black OnyX 3.47, the Black OnyX 2.84 and the Carbon 4 LED tile with the Brompton Receiver Cards installed) and selecting a frame rate, and colour depth you can see how many tiles you can connect to each port. By inputting the LED walls width and height in tiles, and the number of tiles you want to run on the processors (knowing the number of tiles per port)  you will be shown how many Processors are required.

name: MyETC: Photometrics
desc:
The MyETC: Photometrics app helps you estimate how an ETC luminaire will perform on stage by providing dynamic photometric data.
MyETC: Photometrics pulls photometry from across a fixture product line and combines it into an intuitive app for all your design and fixture comparison needs.

Designers, specifiers, and lighting enthusiasts can easily configure and display customized photometrics for each fixture in any color, compare products, and save favorites. Full gel libraries are included with DMX and full spectral values so you can easily reproduce any gel color you’ve used in the past. Select any ETC fixture and its array and quickly get condensed datasheet information.

name: Octopus
desc: Octopus is show control software with the capability to control devices via TCP/IP, UDP, HTTP, Serial and MIDI. Octopus uses Preset’s with a Preset list to build a control interface.

name: PA Bible
desc:
PA Bible is the instant reference guide for any sound engineer.
Technical specifications for hundreds of loudspeakers compiled into one simple, easy to use app.
NOW – data is stored in ‘The Cloud’ – meaning we can add speakers easily! Expect more frequent updates!
All loudspeakers shown with a picture for easy reference and identification.

name: Pattern Generator
desc:
Pattern Generator, allows you to create test patterns that in any resolution.

Patterns available:
Blendind (for wide screen).
LED wall.
Grid with or without circles.
Spyder.
75% Color Bar
100% color bars.
Horizontal 75% Color Bar.
Horizontal Color Bar 100%.
SMPTE color bar.
Horizontal grayscale.

name: piXledPro
desc: piXled Pro is the perfect companion for any video technician specialized or not in LED screens.

name: Plug Finder
desc:
Ever wondered what all those connections are on your new TV or computer?

Or what all those cables you have are supposed to do anyway?

Then this is the app for you!

Plug Finder is the near-effortless reference app that assists you in finding what plugs and cables do, and what they look like.  There are two ways of accomplishing this: Either flick through the intuitive plug viewer to determine the right plug, or scroll through the names and symbols to quickly find what you are looking for.

name: ProjectorCalc
desc:
ProjectorCalc is a program for professional lighting technicians and projectionists. Its primary purpose is to determine the size of a projected image given various information about the projector and it's location.

ProjectorCalc allows users to enter details for a projector and its location in relation to a projection surface.

ProjectorCalc will calculate image width, image height, diagonal length, brightness in Nits, total projected area, and how many pixels per inch the projected image will be.

Additionally, ProjectorCalc can give you an estimate of how much keystone distortion will occur by calculating the length of each side of the distorted image.

name: Projectionist
desc:
Projectionst is a lens calculator for the professional A/V Technician. 

It also contains a detailed product specification database of most common professional high end projectors from Barco, Christie digital, Projection Design, Panasonic, Sanyo and more are included. 

The app can be used to calculate projector lens & distance at a given screen size, or calculate screen size from a given distance & lens for a particular projector. In addition many edge blend calculations can be made.

name: RF Calculators
desc:
- Noise Temperature to Noise Figure Converter
- Free Space Path Loss Calculator
- Microstrip Mitred Bend Calculator
- EIRP Calculator
- Radar Range Calculator
- SAR Calculator
- RF Transformer Calculator
- Time Domain Reflectrometry Calculator
- Spurious Free Dynamic Range Calculator
- Antenna Near Field Calculator
- Parabolic Reflector Antenna Gain Calculator
- Friis Transmission Calculator

And many more...

name: Shadow Magic
desc: A virtual light lab, or Black Box theatre for visualizing lighting, color, shadows, and scenery.

name: ShowTool LD
desc:
Simple. Powerful.
Comprehensive suite of Lighting Design and Electrics utilities for theatre and film.

name: ShowTool Swatch
desc: ShowTool Swatch brings your gel swatch book to life. It's the easiest and most beautiful way of viewing the information most important to you, helping you to make the right decisions when time matters. Share your ideas with friends or send orders straight to your local dealer.

name: SignalScope X
desc: Faber Acoustical introduces SignalScope X as its new flagship dynamic signal analysis, data acquisition, and acoustics app for iOS. The culmination of nearly 10 years of iOS development experience, SignalScope X offers in-app subscriptions to replace and exceed the functionality previously found in other apps from Faber Acoustical, including SignalScope, which was available in the iPhone App Store on its opening day in 2008. SignalScope X is now available on the App Store as a free download.

name: SoundMeter X
desc: SoundMeter, the original sound level meter app for iPhone, has now become SoundMeter X, which can be downloaded for free from the App Store. SoundMeter X offers standard and professional tools and features through affordable auto-renewable subscriptions. It also offers a multi-channel data recorder for capturing raw sounds from one or more microphones connected to your iOS device.

name: Stagehand
desc: Stagehand brings the Roscolux, GAM, Lee and Apollo swatch books together on your iOS device! Gels are viewable by their swatch book, or all together sorted by hue, letting you find the perfect color without worrying about manufacturer. Each gel is complete with manufacturer information including transmission and description as well as samples of neighboring colors in the swatch book.

name: The Audio Toolbox
desc: Comprehensive audio reference and calculation toolkit for sound engineers. Includes SPL calculators, delay time calculators, frequency charts, Ohm's Law, and level conversion tools. A practical all-in-one reference app for audio professionals working in live sound, studio, and installation.

name: WideScreen Pro
desc: The entire collection of gels in Stagehand is searchable not only by its name, number, and description, but also by color with the built-in color search that allows you to select a color from the color wheel and see the closest matches available to the selected color.

name: VideoCalc
desc: Video format and technical specification calculator for video engineers and DIT operators. Calculates frame rates, aspect ratios, data rates, and storage requirements for common video formats. Useful for pre-production planning and on-set media management decisions.

name: AudioCalc
desc: Stagehand is provided as a free reference for professionals and amateurs alike, and we hope you enjoy the app! Stagehand is already used hundreds of times a day and has been downloaded by over 35,000 users since it launched almost 10 years ago!

name: ISF Greyscale Comparator
desc:
"Better HDTV Color Fidelity is a Remote Click Away - Simply compare the Grayscale on this App to the same one on your TV!"
Black and White color accuracy is the foundation of all color accuracy.
Using this application, compare the three step grayscale on your Apple device to the same grayscale viewed on your HDTV or to a similar grayscale from a generator. Use this tool for visual verification of your calibration.

name: What's the Frequency?
desc:
What's the Frequency? will help you to become a better audio engineer. It helps you to recognise the frequencies you are hearing.

Given short audio samples of pure tones or filtered pink noise you have to respond with the correct frequency of the tone, or the center frequency of the filtered pink noise. Go for 100% accuracy.

Start out easy, with octave spacing. When you feel more confident, move up to third octave spacing for a more challenging game.

Use What's the Frequency? for just a few minutes each day and you will notice an improvement in your ability to identify and label the frequencies in the sounds you hear. You'll become more efficient with an EQ. Your mixes are likely to improve. Most of all, you'll win the respect and admiration of fellow audio nerds.

name: Photomath
desc: Math problem solver that uses your camera to scan and solve equations instantly. Useful for quick on-site electrical and cable calculations. Covers arithmetic, algebra, and basic formulas needed for AV power and impedance work.

name: SW array
desc: Speaker array simulation and loudspeaker coverage planning tool. Lets you model directional dispersion patterns for line arrays and point source systems. Helps audio engineers plan system design and predict coverage before deployment.

name: Pinouts
desc: Comprehensive reference guide for connector pinouts across audio, video, data, and power cables. Covers XLR, HDMI, DisplayPort, RJ45, and hundreds of other connectors used in AV production. An essential quick-reference tool for techs in the field.

name: Quiztones
desc: EQ ear training app for audio engineers using real-world tones, filtered noise, and music samples. Helps you learn to identify specific frequencies by ear through progressive difficulty levels. Regularly used by FOH and monitor engineers to sharpen their EQ instincts.

name: Knot 3D
desc: 3D animated knot-tying guide covering dozens of knots with step-by-step visual instructions. Essential reference for rigging professionals, stage crews, and anyone working with ropes and tie-off points in live event production. Helps ensure correct knot selection for different load and safety requirements.

name: Touch OSC
desc: Customizable OSC/MIDI control surface that turns your phone or tablet into a flexible control interface for live production. Design custom button layouts, faders, and rotary controls that send OSC or MIDI messages to audio consoles, lighting desks, and media servers. Widely used for custom remote control workflows in professional AV environments.

name: Lemur
desc: Advanced multi-touch MIDI and OSC controller for live performance, studio use, and show control. Create fully custom control interfaces with a powerful visual editor supporting complex interactions and scripting. A professional-grade remote control solution used by audio engineers, lighting designers, and media server operators.

name: Pocket Light Meter
desc: Light meter app that uses the device camera to measure exposure and lux levels. Useful for basic lighting checks and camera exposure reference on set. A practical quick-reference tool before deploying a dedicated light meter.

name: Fing
desc: Network scanner and device discovery tool for diagnosing AV-over-IP and networked AV setups. Quickly identifies all devices on a network, checks connectivity, and runs ping and port scan tests. Essential for troubleshooting Dante, NDI, and AV-over-IP installations.

name: Smart tools
desc: All-in-one measurement toolkit including sound meter, ruler, protractor, and level. Useful for quick on-site measurements without carrying separate instruments. Includes a decibel meter and vibration detector relevant to audio and staging work.

name: Lucidchart
desc: Professional diagramming tool used for AV signal flow charts, rack layouts, and system design. Supports real-time collaboration so teams can work on technical drawings together. Integrates with Google Drive and Microsoft Office for easy document management.

name: Spectrum analyzer
desc: Real-time audio spectrum analyzer for EQ analysis and room acoustic tuning. Displays frequency content visually so engineers can identify problem areas in a mix or room. A useful complement to a measurement mic for quick field diagnostics.

name: Beecam light meter app
desc: Light meter app designed specifically for cinematographers and video production lighting work. Measures incident and reflected light to help achieve consistent exposure across multi-camera setups. Helpful for video techs evaluating lighting conditions at event venues.

name: SoundGym
desc:
The Gym for your Ears
Get audio ear training online, improve core listening skills like frequency detection or compression, and start sounding like a pro. Anytime, anywhere. Let's start training!

name: E2 EDID
desc: EDID management and analysis tool for Barco E2 video processing workflows.

name: Stream Deck Mobile
desc: Turns a phone or tablet into a customizable control surface for Stream Deck actions.

name: RTA AUDIO
desc: Real-time audio spectrum analyzer for sound system tuning and analysis.

name: LEDWallCalc
desc: Calculates LED wall dimensions, resolution, and pixel mapping for video displays.

name: NDI HX CAM
desc: Uses a mobile device as an NDI HX video source for live production systems.

name: AUX B
desc: Remote control app for AUX and audio routing in professional sound setups.

name: piXedid
desc: EDID editor and generator for configuring display resolutions and formats.

name: pixl Grid
desc: Pixel mapping and visualization tool for LED and video wall layouts.

name: Time Card Calculator-Timeclock
desc: Calculates work hours, overtime, and timecard totals.

name: Stage Invaders
desc: Simple arcade-style game themed around stage and production visuals.

name: MagicQ Remote Control
desc: Remote control interface for ChamSys MagicQ lighting consoles.

name: DMX Calc
desc: DMX addressing and channel calculation tool for lighting systems.

name: Ohms Law Calc
desc: Electrical calculator for voltage, current, resistance, and power using Ohm’s Law.

name: Blackmagic Camera
desc: Professional camera control app with cinematic recording features for mobile devices.

name: FidoLED
desc: LED testing and troubleshooting tool for checking color, brightness, and patterns.

name: Sting Pad
desc: Control surface app for triggering cues, sounds, or lighting actions.

name: Frequency: Sound Generator
desc: Generates test tones and frequencies for audio testing and calibration.

name: GenR8
desc: Generates common test signals and patterns for AV and display testing.

name: ResEditor
desc: Creates and edits custom display resolutions for video and LED systems.

name: Screen Mirror
desc: Mirrors screen

name: VLC
desc: Universal open-source media player that supports nearly every video and audio format without additional codecs. Widely used in AV production for playback testing, format verification, and streaming. A must-have utility for any AV technician's toolkit.

name: TestCardProHD
desc: Generates professional broadcast-quality test cards, color bars, and geometric patterns for display calibration. Used to verify signal integrity, color accuracy, and geometry on video displays and projectors. Supports 4K output and a variety of standard test patterns.

name: Inet
desc: Professional network utility featuring port scanning, ping, traceroute, and device discovery for AV-over-IP diagnostics. Helps AV techs identify network congestion, discover connected devices, and troubleshoot Dante, AVB, and NDI network issues. A go-to networking companion on the show floor.

name: Lazy Lighting
desc: Quick DMX fixture setup and channel calculation tool for lighting designers and technicians. Helps calculate DMX start addresses, channel counts, and universe assignments for fixtures. Speeds up the patching process on shows with large fixture counts.

name: PTZ Control 2
desc: Professional PTZ camera control app for pan, tilt, zoom, and preset management of production cameras. Supports popular PTZOptics, Sony, and other IP-based PTZ cameras used in corporate AV, houses of worship, and broadcast environments. Enables remote camera operation from any position in the venue.

name: IPCamera
desc: Turns a mobile device into a live IP camera viewable over a local network. Useful for creating quick confidence monitor feeds, backstage video monitoring, or remote crew visibility without dedicated camera hardware. A low-cost solution for basic production monitoring needs.

name: V-8HD remote
desc: Official remote control app for the Roland V-8HD HDMI video switcher. Control video switching, transitions, and effects directly from your iPhone or iPad via Wi-Fi. Ideal for single-operator setups where the switcher needs to be positioned away from the operator.

name: v-160 Remote
desc: Official remote control app for the Roland V-160HD streaming video switcher. Provides wireless control of switching, streaming settings, and scene management from a mobile device. Designed for corporate AV environments where the switcher is rack-mounted away from the operator.

name: V-1HD Remote
desc: Official remote control app for the Roland V-1HD portable HD video switcher. Enables wireless control of switching, transitions, and effects from an iPhone or iPad. Great for small corporate events where a compact, one-person operation is required.

name: Mix Effect
desc: Feature-rich ATEM switcher control app with macro management, audio mixing, and multi-viewer support. Provides a polished interface for controlling Blackmagic Design ATEM switchers remotely from iPad or iPhone. A popular professional alternative to the native ATEM Software Control panel.

name: Teams
desc: Microsoft Teams is a collaboration and video conferencing platform widely integrated into corporate AV environments. Used for hybrid event production, remote presenter management, and internal crew communication on live events. Supports video calls, screen sharing, and integration with AV presentation systems.

name: Zoom
desc: Industry-leading video conferencing platform used extensively in corporate AV and hybrid event production. Supports high-quality video meetings, webinars, and screen sharing with large audiences. A core tool for any AV professional working in the corporate events or streaming space.

name: Qu-Pad
desc: Official remote control app for Allen & Heath Qu-series digital mixing consoles. Allows wireless mixing from anywhere in the venue via Wi-Fi, giving engineers freedom to walk the room while adjusting levels. Supports full mixing, EQ, dynamics, and routing control.

name: OBS Blade
desc: Remote control app for OBS Studio that lets you manage live streaming from a mobile device. Control scenes, sources, transitions, and recording status without touching the main computer. Useful for live event operators who need to move around while managing a stream.

name: Video viewer pro
desc: Multi-stream video viewer for monitoring multiple video feeds simultaneously on one screen. Supports RTSP, RTMP, and HTTP streams from IP cameras and video encoders. Useful for multi-camera productions where a single operator needs visibility across all feeds.

name: Touch portal
desc: Customizable macro control panel that functions as a Stream Deck alternative for production and show control. Create button grids that trigger hotkeys, scripts, MIDI, and OSC commands from your phone or tablet. Widely used by AV operators to streamline repetitive production tasks.

name: LED Banner Pro
desc: Scrolling LED banner and marquee display app for signage, announcements, and event use. Easy to configure with custom text, colors, and scroll speed for use on phones or tablets as on-stage signage. A quick, low-cost solution for displaying cue names, song titles, or event information.

name: Media Convert
desc: Video and audio file format converter for quick transcoding on the go. Supports a wide range of input and output formats including MP4, MOV, MP3, WAV, and more. Useful when files need to be converted on-site to match playback system requirements.

name: AB Buddy
desc: A/B audio comparison tool for critical listening, mix evaluation, and speaker system assessment. Allows quick switching between two audio sources to identify differences in EQ, dynamics, and tonal balance. Useful for system techs comparing different setups or making alignment decisions.

name: catch n' sync
desc: Multi-device video synchronization tool for playing back content in sync across multiple screens or devices. Enables precise time-locked playback for multi-screen installations, video walls, and distributed display systems. A practical solution when hardware sync is not available.

name: Audio Training EQ and Feedback
desc: Interactive ear training app for audio engineers focusing on EQ identification and feedback frequency recognition. Presents audio samples at specific frequencies and challenges users to identify them by ear. A practical daily training tool for FOH and monitor engineers looking to sharpen their EQ instincts.

name: Frequency Sound Generator
desc: Generates precise test tones and frequency sweeps for audio system testing and speaker calibration. Supports sine waves, pink noise, and frequency sweeps across the full audible spectrum. An essential field tool for signal chain verification, polarity checks, and basic speaker alignment.

name: TouchMix-30 Control
desc: Official remote control app for the QSC TouchMix-30 Pro digital mixing console. Provides wireless access to all mixing functions including EQ, dynamics, effects, and routing from any Android device. Allows engineers to walk the room and make adjustments without being tied to the console.

name: TouchMix-8/16 Control
desc: Official remote control app for the QSC TouchMix-8 and TouchMix-16 digital mixing consoles. Enables full wireless control of mixing functions, EQ, dynamics, and effects from an Android device. A convenient companion for engineers mixing smaller shows who need freedom to move around the venue.

name: RTA Pro Analyzer
desc: Professional real-time audio spectrum analyzer for sound system tuning and room acoustic measurement. Delivers a continuous high-resolution frequency display with peak hold and averaging capabilities. Used by audio engineers for speaker alignment, system EQ, and diagnosing acoustic problems in venues.

name: TouchOSC Mk1
desc: The original TouchOSC interface editor for building custom OSC and MIDI control surfaces on Android. Design button grids, faders, and XY pads that send messages to audio consoles, lighting desks, and media servers. A powerful and flexible tool for building custom show control interfaces on mobile devices.

name: DM3 StageMix
desc: Official remote mixing app for the Yamaha DM3 series digital mixing console. Provides wireless access to channel faders, EQ, dynamics, and effects from an iPad. Lets engineers walk the stage and mix monitors directly from the performer's position without leaving the console unmanned.

name: Frequency Sound Generator App
desc: iOS app for generating precise test tones and frequency signals for audio testing and speaker calibration. Supports sine, square, triangle, and sawtooth waveforms across the full frequency range. Useful for signal path testing, driver polarity checks, and basic system commissioning in the field.

name: GarageBand
desc: Apple's full-featured digital audio workstation for iPhone and iPad. Includes virtual instruments, multi-track recording, and a loop library — useful for audio production, content creation, and basic editing tasks on the go. A free, powerful tool for AV professionals who also do audio production work.

name: Quiztones: EQ Ear Training
desc: Professional EQ ear training app from The Pro Audio Files using real music samples and filtered tones. Trains you to identify specific frequencies by ear through progressive difficulty levels and daily exercises. Widely used by working audio engineers to sharpen their EQ instincts and improve mix decisions.

name: Sonic Tools SVM
desc: Multi-function audio measurement toolkit for iPhone and iPad covering SPL metering, spectrum analysis, and signal generation. Provides professional-grade acoustic measurement capabilities in a single app. A practical all-in-one tool for audio engineers doing quick system checks and venue assessments.

name: SoundMeter Pro 2022
desc: Professional sound level meter app from Faber Acoustical with calibration support and data logging. Measures SPL in real time with A, C, and Z weighting options and produces detailed reports. Used by audio engineers and venue managers for compliance monitoring and acoustic documentation.

name: Sound Check Helper
desc: Online tool to assist audio engineers with sound check workflows and frequency reference charts. Helps streamline the pre-show process with checklists, signal flow references, and frequency identification guides. A quick browser-based companion for audio techs preparing for a show.

name: Elgato Stream Deck Mobile
desc: Turns your phone or tablet into a fully customizable Elgato Stream Deck control surface. Trigger macros, switch scenes in OBS or vMix, control audio, and automate production workflows from any device on the same network. A flexible and cost-effective alternative or companion to a physical Stream Deck.

name: Keuwlsoft's Apps for Android
desc: Collection of free Android utility apps including a function generator, frequency counter, oscilloscope, and audio tools. Useful for signal testing, frequency measurement, and basic audio diagnostics in the field. A handy toolkit for AV techs who need quick measurement tools on their Android device.

name: Smart Tools - All In One Box
desc: Comprehensive measurement toolkit for Android including sound meter, ruler, level, protractor, and metal detector. Provides a wide range of on-site measurement capabilities without needing separate instruments. A practical everyday utility for AV and staging technicians working in the field.

name: AV Buddy Pro
desc: Professional upgrade to AV Buddy with expanded calculators, pinouts, reference charts, and AV-over-IP references. Covers connector pinouts, cable lengths, dB conversion, aspect ratios, and networking calculators in one app. An indispensable field reference tool for AV technicians on any type of production.

name: XTEN-AV Online Calculators
desc: Suite of free online AV calculation tools from XTEN-AV covering cable, signal, power, and system design calculations. Includes throw distance calculators, audio coverage estimators, and network bandwidth calculators. A useful browser-based resource for AV system designers and integrators.

name: piXled Pro
desc: Professional LED panel configuration and pixel mapping tool for Android. Calculates pixel counts, resolution, and signal requirements for LED video wall setups. A practical companion for LED video technicians planning and commissioning LED wall installations.

name: Absen Panel Calulator
desc: Online LED panel calculator from Absen for determining resolution, pixel pitch, viewing distance, and power requirements for Absen LED products. Helps sales engineers and technicians spec the right panel configuration for a given application. Useful for AV integrators working with Absen LED systems.

name: DMX (DIP Switch) Calculator
desc: Android app for calculating DMX start addresses and DIP switch configurations for lighting fixtures. Enter a DMX address and instantly see the correct DIP switch pattern, or read an existing pattern to find the address. Eliminates manual calculation errors during fixture patching on large lighting rigs.

name: CueTrack for Eos® Family consoles
desc: Cue management and tracking app for ETC Eos family lighting consoles. Allows lighting programmers to review, annotate, and manage cue lists from an iPad without needing to be at the console. A useful companion for lighting designers and board operators working on complex cueing sessions.

name: Gel Swatch Library
desc: Complete digital gel swatch library for Rosco, Lee, GAM, and Apollo color filters. Browse, compare, and search gel colors by name, number, or color family directly from your phone. An essential reference for lighting designers and programmers working with color in theatrical and live event production.

name: ShadowMagic
desc: Virtual lighting lab and gobo visualization tool for theatrical and architectural lighting design. Allows lighting designers to experiment with gobo projections, color mixing, and shadow effects before loading into a real fixture. A creative pre-visualization tool that saves programming time on the show floor.

name: ShowPlay
desc: Simple cue playback and show management app for lighting operators using ETC Eos family consoles. Provides a clean, easy-to-use interface for running pre-programmed cue lists during live performances. Designed for operators who need a reliable, distraction-free tool during show execution.

name: Fing - Network Tools
desc: Professional network scanner and diagnostic tool for identifying devices, checking connectivity, and troubleshooting network issues. Quickly discovers all devices on a network with IP addresses, MAC addresses, and device names. An essential tool for AV technicians working with Dante, NDI, and AV-over-IP systems.

name: Ohms Law Calculator
desc: Clean and fast Ohm's Law calculator for Android covering voltage, current, resistance, and power calculations. Enter any two known values and the app instantly calculates the remaining two. A quick everyday reference for AV and power technicians doing electrical calculations in the field.

name: DWG FastView-CAD Viewer&Editor
desc: Professional CAD file viewer and editor for Android supporting DWG, DXF, and DWF formats. Allows AV technicians and integrators to view architectural drawings, signal flow diagrams, and system schematics on a mobile device. Useful for referencing venue drawings and system layouts on the show floor.

name: ShowTool SM
desc: Stage management and show tracking app for theatre and live event production. Manages running orders, cue sheets, contact lists, and production notes from a single iPad interface. A reliable organizational tool for stage managers, ASMs, and production coordinators running complex shows.

name: Knotts 3D
desc: 3D animated knot reference app with step-by-step visual instructions for dozens of knots. Essential for rigging professionals, stage crews, and anyone working with ropes, slings, and tie-off points in live event production. Helps ensure correct knot selection for different load conditions and safety requirements.

name: Rigging Bridle Calculator+
desc: Rigging bridle load and angle calculator for live event production professionals. Calculates bridle leg tensions, pickup point forces, and safe working loads based on span, trim height, and load weight. A critical safety tool for riggers planning overhead load configurations on tours and events.

name: Light Meter - Lite
desc: Light meter app for Android using the device camera to measure lux and foot-candle levels. Provides quick exposure and lighting level readings without needing a dedicated hardware light meter. Useful for video techs and lighting directors assessing ambient light conditions at event venues.

name: MixEffect (Trial & Pro Unlock)
desc: Full-featured Blackmagic Design ATEM switcher control app for iPhone and iPad with macro management, audio mixing, and multi-viewer support. Provides a polished, professional interface with features beyond the stock ATEM Software Control panel. Widely used by live production operators working with ATEM switchers on events and broadcasts.

name: WScreenPros
desc: Widescreen and aspect ratio calculator for video professionals and AV technicians. Quickly calculates screen dimensions, throw distances, and display configurations for different aspect ratios and resolutions. A practical reference tool for video techs and system designers working with projection and LED screen setups.

name: Pro Filmmaker Apps for all platforms
desc: Curated directory of professional filmmaking and video production apps organized by category and platform. Covers camera control, color grading, audio, production management, and more across iOS, Android, and desktop. A useful discovery resource for video professionals looking for the best tools for their workflow.

name: Kodi
desc: Open-source media center and sample library platform used widely in AV production for testing and playback. The Kodi wiki hosts a curated library of free video and audio sample files useful for display calibration, codec testing, and content verification. A go-to resource for video technicians who need standardized test content across different playback systems.
